title: BUG: did:web domain encoding replaces dots with colons (wrong per W3C spec)
description: In src/did.rs line 30, `domain.replace('.', ":")` produces `did:web:example:com:users:alice` but the W3C DID Web Method spec says dots in the domain remain as-is. The correct output is `did:web:example.com:users:alice`. Colons in did:web separate path segments, not domain components. The domain is used verbatim (dots and all). Port numbers use percent-encoding (`%3A`). This bug affects: the DID document `id` field, the actor JSON `alsoKnownAs`, and the `did recover` lookup. The test on line 174 asserts the WRONG expected value. Fix: remove the `.replace('.', ":")` call. Update the test to expect `did:web:example.com:users:alice`.
